name: Veal Oscar with Sauce Bernaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Flatten the veal cutlets to an even thickness using a mallet, heavy knife, or rolling pin (a 2 x 4 works well). Season both sides with salt and black pepper.
2. Dredge each flattened cutlet in flour, shaking off excess.
3. In a large skillet, melt butter over moderate heat. Add the veal cutlets and sauté until golden brown on one side, about 2-3 minutes. Flip and continue cooking until browned on the other side.
4. Transfer the cooked cutlets to a warmed platter.
5. Reduce the beef stock in a saucepan until just simmering. Pour over the veal cutlets.
6. Arrange four asparagus spears on top of each cutlet.
7. Spoon additional sauce bernaise over the asparagus and drizzle over the cutlets.
8. Serve 6 portions.
Sauce Bernaise:
1. Combine equal parts wine vinegar and dry white wine in a saucepan.
2. Add minced shallots or green onions (8 tablespoons), fresh tarragon or dried tarragon (4 teaspoons).
3. Stir in equal amounts of chopped parsley and chives (8 tablespoons each), salt, and black pepper to taste.
4. Simmer until the mixture reduces by two-thirds.
